evals.prompt: What dropped since last time? Alert me on any significant ranking or traffic changes.
evals.expected_output: Should auto-detect a source with at least two snapshots (preferring merged), find the two newest, diff them, and report alerts grouped by severity (critical / warning / info). Saves a dated JSON report and uses 0 NodesHub tokens.
assertions: Auto-detects the source when --source is not given (prefers merged, then rank, then visibility)
Finds the two newest snapshots for the chosen source
Groups alerts by severity (critical / warning / info)
Each alert shows the entity, before -> after, and the delta

evals.prompt: Run the alerting demo and show me the drops and gains.
evals.expected_output: Should run alert.py --demo against the bundled in-script merged snapshots, print a severity-grouped table with both traffic drops (a URL losing all clicks, a query down 60%) and gains (a query traffic increase, a position improvement), and write the JSON report.
assertions: Runs with the --demo flag (no real snapshots needed)
Surfaces a critical alert for clicks falling to zero
Surfaces at least one traffic-drop alert above the default 25% threshold
Surfaces at least one info gain (traffic up or position improved)
Does not call the NodesHub API

evals.prompt: Monitor ranking drops for example.com from my rank-tracker history, threshold 5 positions.
evals.expected_output: Should compare the two newest rank-history snapshots for example.com with --rank-threshold 5, flag keywords that worsened by 5+ positions or fell out of the top 10 as critical/warning, surface big gains as info, and exit cleanly with a 'need at least two snapshots' message if only one exists.
assertions: Flags lost top rankings as critical and other drops as warning
Exits cleanly with a clear message when fewer than two snapshots exist (no fabrication)
